criteria.description: Native acceptance boundary defines compatible operation, payload, status, metrics, and failure behavior without requiring a compiled extension.
criteria.evidence: WP01 approved with reviewed lane-a commit d90ac31. Evidence: tests/Runtime/NativeAcceptance/NativeRuntimeAcceptanceTest.php, tests/Runtime/NativeAcceptance/NativeRuntimeContractAssertions.php, docs/enterprise/native-runtime-acceptance.md; composer check passed in lane-a.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: native acceptance fixture and bridge tests pass under Composer mode.

criteria.description: PHP-level compatibility fixture can be reused by a future native implementation.
criteria.evidence: WP01 approved with reviewed lane-a commit d90ac31. Native acceptance assertions are reusable against injected compatible invokers and do not require a compiled extension; composer check passed in lane-a.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: fixture accepts an injected invoker rather than hard-coding PhpExtensionBridge internals.

criteria.description: Sidecar durable resume request and response contract is defined with versioned sidecar envelopes.
criteria.evidence: WP02 approved with reviewed lane-b commit fc09d5e. Evidence: SidecarResumeRequest, SidecarResumeResponse, SidecarResumeClient, SidecarTransport, and sidecar tests; composer check passed in lane-b.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: request/response tests assert envelope type, run ID, action, status, and metadata.

criteria.description: Durable resume behavior is demonstrated without running a sidecar process.
criteria.evidence: WP02 approved with reviewed lane-b commit fc09d5e. Evidence: php examples/runtime/durable-sidecar-resume.php passed and focused tests/Runtime/Sidecar passed without a running sidecar process.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: fake or injectable transport example runs locally.

criteria.description: Enterprise adapter split decision records first package candidate, ownership boundary, likely future file movement, and timing rationale.
criteria.evidence: WP03 approved with reviewed lane-c commit 9341198. Evidence: docs/architecture/001-enterprise-adapter-split.md chooses purple-php/provider-bedrock, records ownership, future file movement, and deferred extraction rationale.

criteria.description: Composer mode remains the stable baseline and validates without native extension or running sidecar.
criteria.evidence: WP04 approved with reviewed lane-d commit 1920c81. Evidence: composer check passed, deployment readiness and SDK baseline tests pass, and sidecar/native requirements remain optional.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: composer check passes and deployment readiness keeps native runtime optional.

criteria.description: Roadmap and enterprise docs represent Phase 5.1 as runtime continuation work while preserving optional-native strategy.
criteria.evidence: WP03 and WP04 approved with reviewed commits 9341198 and 1920c81. Evidence: ADR plus README, outline, and docs/enterprise/README.md preserve Composer-first and optional-native language.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: README, outline, and enterprise docs align on Composer-first wording.

negative_invariants.description: No test, example, or default SDK path may require a compiled native extension.

negative_invariants.description: No test, example, or default SDK path may require a running sidecar service.
This mission must not physically split Composer packages.
